_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **MONITORING — Track Ministerial gazettal status immediately.** Amendment C376 was adopted by the City of Melbourne Council on 29 July 2025 and has been submitted to the Victorian Minister for Planning for final approval and gazettal. Under Victorian planning law, an amendment only becomes operative upon gazettal in the Victoria Government Gazette. As of the date of this brief (May 2026), gazettal has not yet been confirmed. The workspace should actively monitor the Victorian Government Gazette and the Participate Melbourne portal weekly for notice of approval. Once gazetting occurs, all new planning applications lodged after that date will be subject to the new DDO73 mandatory standards with no transition grace period for new permits. *Source: Amendment C376: Sustainable Building Design, City of Melbourne / Participate Melbourne, 29 July 2025. https://participate.melbourne.vic.gov.au/amendment-c376*

- **ACTION REQUIRED — Assess all Melbourne development projects in the pipeline against C376 mandatory thresholds before lodging planning permits.** Any workspace client or joint-venture with a development of 1,000 sqm+ gross floor area (GFA) in City of Melbourne municipality that has not yet lodged a planning permit will be subject to the full suite of mandatory BESS, NatHERS, Green Star, urban heat island, waste, water, and Green Factor requirements once gazettal occurs. Projects approaching the permit-lodgement stage should be reviewed against Schedule 73 to the Design and Development Overlay (DDO73) before submission to avoid conditions or refusals. Cost consequence: redesign at tender or post-permit stage carries significant cost uplift. *Source: How does this affect me? | Amendment C376, Participate Melbourne, 29 July 2025. https://participate.melbourne.vic.gov.au/amendment-c376/how-does-this-affect-me*

- **WINDOW CLOSING — For projects targeting pre-C376 permit lodgement, any application must be submitted prior to gazettal date.** Once the Minister gazettes the amendment, all new planning applications are captured. Developments that can be lodge-ready before gazettal are not subject to the new mandatory ESD standards. The workspace should advise any Melbourne clients with near-ready projects to lodge without delay. *Source: Amendment C376: Sustainable Building Design, Participate Melbourne, 29 July 2025. https://participate.melbourne.vic.gov.au/amendment-c376*

- **MONITORING — Engage a suitably qualified ESD consultant now for any in-scope Melbourne development.** Large developments (>5,000 sqm) must provide a Sustainability Management Plan (SMP); smaller new buildings require a Sustainable Design Assessment (SDA). The workspace must engage environmentally sustainable design consultants as part of permit preparation; Council technical assistance is available on request but no financial assistance is offered. *Source: Amendment C376: Sustainable Building Design, Participate Melbourne, 29 July 2025. https://participate.melbourne.vic.gov.au/amendment-c376*
